transcript.clean_text: The final stage of the Tour of Romandie, an individual time trial 15.84 km starting in Aigle and ending up in Villars. The opening first few kilometers very flat before commencing a nasty climb. 11.3 K's at 7.4% maxing out at 12.7. This was going to be a very very stern test. This is where the race would indeed be decided. There's a profile and 850 m of elevation gain. Steven Kruijswijk was out on course, a teammate of the race leader Rohan Dennis and looking very strong indeed. 17 seconds quicker at the first intermediate split. And heading into the finish line was to beat the time set by Thibaut Pinot by just under 3 seconds. Steven Kruijswijk briefly ahead in the leaderboard. Superb performance by the rider from the Netherlands. But next up Damiano Caruso, the Sicilian storming up the finishing straight to set a new fastest time by 3/4 of a second. One of a ride by the man from Bahrain Victorious. Simon Geschke of Cofidis, the German, started the day in ninth place overall but with this ride was going to move himself onto the podium. What a performance it was. Over 33 seconds quicker than the time of Damiano Caruso. He was the fastest on the course at that point. There was also another superb performance by young Gino Mäder. The Swiss rider riding for Bahrain Victorious with this performance was heading on to the overall podium. Just 4 seconds slower than Simon Geschke and good enough at that point for second place. Third man off the ramp from the end was Aleksandr Vlasov. Started the day 18 seconds behind the race leader Rohan Dennis. And the man from Bora-Hansgrohe was soon into his rhythm. Started off on the TT bike, bike change at the bottom of the climb. And then off on his road bike. And the final man off the start ramp, Rohan Dennis, the race leader, the Australian TT champion, also opting like many others for a bike change at the bottom of this ferocious climb. A quick change later and he was on his way. He was to set the third fastest time at the opening intermediate split. But meanwhile further up the mountain Aleksandr Vlasov smashed the time of Simon Geschke to set a new mark of 33:40. Over 30 seconds quicker than the German. Wonderful performance from Aleksandr Vlasov and that was to be the ride of the day. Utterly spent at the end. Difficult difficult TT. 100% given by everybody on the day including this man, Juan Ayuso. He was to fall away from the podium spot but a very respectable ride by the best young rider, only 19 years of age. Rohan Dennis, focused. He did fade out towards the back end, set the third fastest intermediate split but at the end was over 2 minutes shy of the time set by Aleksandr Vlasov but you could not fault this man's effort. Emptied the tank completely. 100% effort from the Australian champion. So stage win went to Aleksandr Vlasov ahead of Simon Geschke, Gino Mäder, Caruso, Kruijswijk, Pinot, Talansky, Ayuso, Kuss and Ben O'Connor. Thumbs up from the man of Bora-Hansgrohe. It was to be his first ever World Tour win. So the overall this 75th edition of this race, Vlasov took the win ahead of Mäder, Geschke, Ayuso, O'Connor, Caruso, Kruijswijk, Dennis, Plapp and Einer Rubio. So celebrations all round. The final green jersey of race leader to Aleksandr Vlasov ahead of a delighted Gino Mäder and also a smiling Simon Geschke of Cofidis. It's been a magnificent edition of this race. Thanks very much for joining us. We'll see you soon.
